fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable with a high valuation and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY, with analyst estimates projecting 109.4% for the current quarter and 441.7% for the current fiscal year.
Forward P/E of 37.68, while high, is lower than the PEG ratio of 0.78, suggesting growth may outpace valuation.
Strong current and quick ratios (3.83 and 2.85 respectively) indicate good short-term liquidity.
Total cash of $439.70M provides a cushion against short-term debt of $280.42M.
negatives: Operating and profit margins are negative (-8.6% and -8.5% respectively), indicating the company is not profitable.
Return on equity and assets are also negative (-6.1% and -3.3%), showing poor efficiency in generating returns.
High Debt/Equity ratio of 25.36 suggests significant leverage.
Free cash flow is negative (-$445.58M TTM), indicating cash burn.
Last reported quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention, with articles highlighting its substantial 6-month stock performance and large transceiver orders, while also questioning if the current valuation is sustainable. Despite a recent earnings miss and negative margins, the company is expected to see explosive growth in the current quarter and fiscal year, driven by AI demand. The stock's high beta and significant run-up suggest volatility, with analysts divided on its future prospects.
